2604.19341.title: Evaluation-driven Scaling for Scientific Discovery
2604.19341.paper_summary: Language models are increasingly used in scientific discovery to generate hypotheses, propose candidate solutions, implement systems, and iteratively refine them. At the core of these trial-and-error loops lies evaluation: the process of obtaining feedback on candidate solutions via verifiers, simulators, or task-specific scoring functions. While prior work has highlighted the importance of evaluation, it has not explicitly formulated the problem of how evaluation-driven discovery loops can be scaled up in a principled and effective manner to push the boundaries of scientific discovery, a problem this paper seeks to address. We introduce Simple Test-time Evaluation-driven Scaling (SimpleTES), a general framework that strategically combines parallel exploration, feedback-driven refinement, and local selection, revealing substantial gains unlocked by scaling evaluation-driven discovery loops along the right dimensions. Across 21 scientific problems spanning six domains, SimpleTES discovers state-of-the-art solutions using gpt-oss models, consistently outperforming both frontier-model baselines and sophisticated optimization pipelines. Particularly, we sped up the widely used LASSO algorithm by over 2x, designed quantum circuit routing policies that reduce gate overhead by 24.5%, and discovered new Erdos minimum overlap constructions that surpass the best-known results. Beyond novel discoveries, SimpleTES produces trajectory-level histories that naturally supervise feedback-driven learning. When post-trained on successful trajectories, models not only improve efficiency on seen problems but also generalize to unseen problems, discovering solutions that base models fail to uncover. Together, our results establish effective evaluation-driven loop scaling as a central axis for advancing LLM-driven scientific discovery, and provide a simple yet practical framework for realizing these gains.
2604.19341.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型在科學發現中的應用越來越廣泛，用於生成假設、提出候選解決方案、實施系統並進行迭代改進。這些試錯循環的核心在於評估：通過驗證者、模擬器或特定任務的評分函數獲取對候選解決方案的反饋過程。雖然先前的研究強調了評估的重要性，但並未明確提出如何以原則性和有效的方式擴展以評估為驅動的發現循環，以推動科學發現的邊界，這是本文所要解決的問題。我們介紹了簡單測試時評估驅動擴展（SimpleTES），這是一個通用框架，戰略性地結合了平行探索、反饋驅動的改進和局部選擇，揭示了沿著正確維度擴展評估驅動發現循環所釋放的實質性增益。在涵蓋六個領域的21個科學問題中，SimpleTES 使用 gpt-oss 模型發現了最先進的解決方案，始終超越了前沿模型基準和複雜的優化管道。特別是，我們將廣泛使用的 LASSO 算法加速了超過 2 倍，設計的量子電路路由策略將閘的開銷減少了 24.5%，並發現了新的 Erdos 最小重疊結構，超越了已知的最佳結果。除了新穎的發現外，SimpleTES 還生成了自然監督反饋驅動學習的軌跡級歷史。在對成功的軌跡進行後訓練時，模型不僅在已見問題上提高了效率，還能對未見問題進行泛化，發現基礎模型無法揭示的解決方案。總體而言，我們的結果確立了有效的評估驅動循環擴展作為推進 LLM 驅動科學發現的核心軸心，並提供了一個簡單而實用的框架來實現這些增益。
2604.19341.author: Haotian Ye et.al.
2604.19341.authors: Haotian Ye, Haowei Lin, Jingyi Tang, Yizhen Luo, Caiyin Yang, Chang Su, Rahul Thapa, Rui Yang, Ruihua Liu, Zeyu Li, Chong Gao, Dachao Ding, Guangrong He, Miaolei Zhang, Lina Sun, Wenyang Wang, Yuchen Zhong, Zhuohao Shen, Di He, Jianzhu Ma, Stefano Ermon, Tongyang Li, Xiaowen Chu, James Zou, Yuzhi Xu
